Document:
# MILITARY PRESENCE Military forces do not normally control civilian criminal law. They may assist during emergencies, evacuations, riots, quarantine events, and colony defense.
Active militaries are requested to transfer civilian suspects from military custody to the Marshal's Office for processing under colonial civil law, unless martial law has been declared. This request is not absolute: military commanders may retain custody of individuals deemed an ongoing threat to military operations or colonial security. Including but not limited to: terrorists, insurgents, saboteurs or persons whose release or transfer would compromise active operations.
Military personnel who commit crimes against civilians may be detained or reported to military police or higher colonial authority. Civilian law remains civilian law unless martial law is lawfully declared.
